title: Lake Geneva Wisconsin Canidates Forum Altercation

communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Wisconsin Open Records Act, I hereby request the following records:

RE: Wisconsin Open Records Request — Ames/Hoiland Altercation, March 18, 2026 — Candidates Debate

Dear Chief 

Pursuant to Wisconsin Statute § 19.35(1)(a) and the Wisconsin Public Records Law, I hereby request access to and copies of all records in the possession, custody, or control of the Lake Geneva Police Department relating to an altercation that occurred on March 18, 2026, between Cheri Ames and Joel Hoiland at or in connection with the Lake Geneva aldermanic candidates debate held that evening.

RECORDS REQUESTED

I request all of the following records without limitation as to form or format:

1. Incident and Police Reports
All initial incident reports, supplemental reports, narratives, and the final investigative report and conclusions prepared by any officer involved in responding to or investigating the March 18, 2026 altercation.

2. Video and Audio Recordings
All video recordings, body-worn camera footage, dashcam recordings, surveillance video, and any other audio or visual recordings captured by Lake Geneva Police Department personnel in connection with this incident, including recordings made at or near the debate venue.

3. Witness and Party Statements
All written, recorded, or transcribed statements obtained from any party, witness, or individual in connection with the investigation of this altercation, including but not limited to statements from Cheri Ames, Joel Hoiland, and civilian witness Joe Esposito.

4. Joe Esposito Witness Statement and Related Records
All records relating to the statement provided by civilian witness Joe Esposito, including his written or recorded statement, any follow-up communications between Mr. Esposito and the Department, and any records reflecting how his account was considered in the investigation.

5. Communications
All internal communications, emails, text messages, radio logs, CAD (Computer Aided Dispatch) records, and dispatch audio relating to the incident, including the initial call for service, officer response, and any subsequent departmental communications regarding the matter.

6. Investigative Notes and Materials
All field notes, investigative memoranda, photographs, and any other documents created or collected in the course of investigating this incident.

7. Final Report and Conclusions
The final police department report, findings, and conclusions resulting from the investigation of this altercation, including any supervisory review or approval of those conclusions.

8. Disposition Records
Any records reflecting the outcome of the investigation, including any referrals to the Walworth County District Attorney's office, charging decisions, declination of prosecution, or other final disposition of the matter.

FORMAT AND DELIVERY

I request that records be provided in electronic format (PDF or native digital format) via email. If any records exist only in physical form, I request legible copies. If electronic delivery is not practicable, please advise and I will arrange for alternative delivery.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 10 business days.

Sincerely,

Gary Milliette

To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Florida Sunshine Law, I hereby request the following records:

Pursuant to the Freedom of Information Act, I respectfully request access to and copies of all records related to the procurement of a school bus stop-arm camera / school bus infraction detection system associated with:

Solicitation/Bid: SDOC-25-P-072-LV

Title/Description: Bus Stop-Arm Violation Program

Issuing Entity: School District of Osceola County

Specifically, I am requesting:

All proposals and responses submitted by all vendors.

The number of buses installed and number of tickets issued from the beginning of the contract for the school bus program.

All contracts, agreements, amendments, addenda, and pricing schedules resulting from this solicitation.

All evaluation materials, including scoring sheets, evaluation committee notes or summaries, and recommendation memoranda.

All bid tabulations, comparison matrices, or award justifications.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 10 business days.

Sincerely,

Jon Smith

title: Records Request ico Christopher Jonathan McClendon

communications.subject: California Public Records Act Request: Records Request ico Christopher Jonathan McClendon
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the California Public Records Act, I hereby request the following records:

Pursuant to the California Public Records Act (California Government Code §§ 7920.000 et seq.) and Article I, § 3(b) of the California Constitution, I request copies of all records in the possession, custody, or control of the Joint Regional Intelligence Center (JRIC) that relate to, reference, mention, or were generated because of the below-named individual.
Subject:
Christopher Jonathan McClendon (also known as Jon McClendon, Christopher J. McClendon, and the following aliases/nicknames):
CHRISTIOPHE JONATHAN MCCLENDON
CHRISTOPHE MCCLENDON
CHRISTOPHER MCCLENDON
CHRISTOPHER JONAT MCCLENDON
CHRISTOPHER JONATHA MCCLENDON
MCCLENDON, CHRISTOPHER JONATHAN
Date of birth: January 5, 1988
Time period: October 26, 2020 to the present (or the most recent date possible).
Records requested include, but are not limited to:
Any Net Assessments, Situational Reports, Advisories, Warnings, Alerts, Bulletins, Threat Assessments, Executive Reports, TLO Reports, or SME Analysis Reports.
Any Suspicious Activity Reports (SARs), tips, leads, or information reports.
Any entries, queries, or records in any JRIC database, watchlist, or intelligence system.
Any records showing that information about the subject was collected, analyzed, stored, or disseminated (including dissemination logs or sharing records with partner agencies).
Any correspondence, emails, memos, or notes concerning the subject or any prior California Public Records Act requests about the subject.
Any records indicating JRIC or its partner agencies conducted surveillance, monitoring, or data inquiries related to the subject.
I request the records in electronic format (native files preferred) and ask that you search all databases, email accounts, shared drives, and systems accessible to JRIC. Please segregate and release any reasonably segregable non-exempt portions of records, as required by law. If any portion is withheld, please provide a detailed written explanation identifying the specific exemption(s) claimed and the name/title of the person making the determination.
Please provide an estimated cost before incurring any fees over $25, and let me know promptly if you need any clarification to process this request.
Thank you for your assistance.


Please note that this is a self-request. The undersigned requester is the subject of the records being sought: Christopher Jonathan McClendon (DOB January 5, 1988), also known by all aliases and nicknames listed above. I am making this request in my individual capacity as the person to whom the records relate.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 10 calendar days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Christopher Mcclendon

communications.subject: Texas Public Information Act Request: McLennan County Administrator Dustin Chapman
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Texas Public Information Act, I hereby request the following records:

All transmissions and records from all conversations or transmission from Chris Abel or Ellis County or any other prosecutor or the elected DA of Ellis county. Emails, letters, memos, or transmission between Josh Tetens and Ranger Bradley Freeman or any Texas Ranger about the McNamara Investigation and/or Ellis County.
Interoffice memos on Ellis County.
Letters, emails, texts between Parnell McNamara and Josh Tetens concerning investigation of McNamara.
Letters, memos, texts, emails between Josh Tetens and any law enforcement entity about McNamara.
Any texts or communications between Josh Tetens and any Ranger about Bernadette Feazell, Berni Feazell, and I'm Mad too, Harry or Harry Storm.
Any communications between Josh Tetens, theh Da's office and Ranger Henderson or any Ranger.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 10 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Bernadette Feazell
